chunk_summary: Guidance on integrating iron-rich foods and national supplementation schedules to address iron deficiency in children aged 6–24 months, emphasizing dietary diversity, vitamin C pairing, and avoiding tea/coffee, alongside prophylactic IFA, vitamin A, and deworming protocols.
learning_objectives: Understand the role of iron-rich anchor foods in complementary feeding for children aged 6–24 months.
Learn the national supplementation schedules for IFA, vitamin A, and deworming under India's health programs.
Identify signs of iron deficiency and appropriate interventions, including dietary improvements and medical treatment.
key_facts: Children 6–59 months receive prophylactic IFA syrup twice weekly (1 mL provides 20 mg elemental iron and 100 mcg folic acid).
Vitamin A supplementation includes 100,000 IU at 9 months and 200,000 IU every 6 months until 5 years (total 9 doses).
National Deworming Day provides albendazole: 200 mg for children 1–2 years and 400 mg for children 2–19 years, twice yearly.
Iron absorption is enhanced by pairing iron-rich foods with vitamin C sources and avoiding tea/coffee in under-twos.
Signs of iron deficiency include pallor, pica, breath-holding spells, developmental concerns, and poor growth.
reference_quote: Under Anemia Mukt Bharat, children 6–59 months receive prophylactic IFA syrup twice weekly (1 mL providing 20 mg elemental iron plus 100 micrograms folic acid).
document_key_topics: Iron-rich anchor foods in complementary feeding
National supplementation schedules for IFA, vitamin A, and deworming
Dietary strategies to enhance iron absorption
Signs and management of iron deficiency in children
